title: phaseolus lathyroides f. typicus
intro: Phaseolus lathyroides f. typicus, also known as the woolly vetch, is a type of legume plant that belongs to the family Fabaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to act as a natural diuretic and laxative, due to its high content of anthraquinone glycosides. The health benefits of woolly vetch include its potential to treat digestive issues, such as constipation and irritable bowel syndrome, as well as its ability to help manage blood sugar levels and reduce inflammation. In herbal preparations, woolly vetch is often used in the form of dried roots or seeds, which can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal use.
